description: Media Session hardening (patch -> v1.2.1), from real device feedback on v1.2.0. IN SCOPE: (1) set + maintain navigator.mediaSession.playbackState and positionState (duration/position/playbackRate), updated on play/pause/ended/seeked/ratechange/timeupdate — likely cause of the lock-screen Now Playing going blank after backgrounding (we set metadata + handlers but never playbackState/positionState). (2) Re-assert Media Session metadata + state on visibilitychange (foreground) so it can't stay stale. (3) iOS rotate-to-fullscreen EXIT: use mediaPlayer.webkitExitFullscreen() (document.exitFullscreen doesn't control iOS native video fullscreen). (4) Document that true background PLAYBACK / resume-after-suspension is an iOS OS limitation for web video we cannot defeat; native PiP is the real background path — this branch hardens the control surface, not background playback. Additive + feature-detected everywhere (no throw where mediaSession/positionState/webkitExitFullscreen absent). iOS-device-only for most of it; only pure logic is node:testable. Significant two-reviewer QA/code-review pass required, focused on feature-detection safety and not regressing the v1.2.0 Media Session / rotate code.

Bootstrapped and staged for discovery. Slug media-session-hardening, patch v1.2.1 hardening the Media Session control surface based on real-device feedback on v1.2.0. Description captured verbatim. No overlapping active exec plans (mobile-playback-polish is a shipped tombstone in active/, real content now in completed/). Tech-debt tracker untouched. Awaiting /prep-pm-discover to route product-manager.

Product-manager discovery complete. Grounded in real code (public/js/watch.js setupMediaSession ~L188-210, setupRotateFullscreen ~L426-460, inNativeFullscreen). Confirmed via grep: no existing playbackState/positionState/visibilitychange/timeupdate/loadedmetadata/ratechange/seeked wiring today, so the gap described is real. docs/exec-plans/active/ is empty (no overlap); no ARCHITECTURE.md contradiction; not a tech-debt-tracker item. Wrote exec plan with explicit HARD CONSTRAINT (background playback is an iOS OS limitation, out of scope/unfixable; PiP is the real path), concrete acceptance criteria for playbackState/positionState (with setPositionState throw-guard requirements)/visibilitychange re-assert/iOS webkitExitFullscreen fix, a testability section recommending an extractable clampPositionState(duration, position, playbackRate) pure helper for node:test coverage, a risks section (setPositionState throw conditions incl. live-transcode/streaming duration edge cases, timeupdate throttling, no double-registration, visibilitychange ordering), and a QA note requiring a significant two-reviewer pass. Artifacts requirements/exec_plan set to docs/exec-plans/active/media-session-hardening.md. Ready for engineering-manager / principal-engineer design.
